description: Tournament harness comparing COMPLETE end-to-end browser agents under self-invented approaches, evaluated on a deliberately hard browser-task suite. Each Ralph iteration either (a) builds harness infrastructure, (b) ships a NEW complete agent under an approach DISTINCT in core mechanism from every agent already present, (c) mines past trajectories for new approach ideas, or (d) advances the tournament. Ralph is encouraged to INVENT its own approaches; the seed paradigms in tasks/prd-general-browser-agent.md §6 (P1–P12) and the P13+ hypotheses are inspiration, NOT a checklist and NOT a constraint. Repeat the open agent slots with novel mechanisms each time. The full PRD lives at tasks/prd-general-browser-agent.md — read it before each iteration. STEERING (2026-05-10): top-level `steeringNotes` field below contains current user-directed priorities — read it BEFORE picking the next user story each iteration. New US-026..U-033 (real-site hard slice, self-hosted complex apps, easy-slice v2, Claude provider, replacement agents for under-performing mechanisms, DOM-as-filesystem) are the live directives; treat them as higher signal than priority numbers if they conflict.

userStories.title: CDP browser pool with per-task isolation and snapshot/restore
userStories.description: As a harness operator, I need N parallel Chrome contexts in a pool with per-task isolation and snapshot/restore so agent runs do not cross-contaminate and rollback-style agents have a substrate.
acceptanceCriteria: BrowserPool (configurable size via GBA_POOL_SIZE, default 4) hands out isolated BrowserSession objects
Each BrowserSession uses a unique --user-data-dir torn down on release
BrowserSession exposes snapshot() and restore(snapshot) using CDP Page.captureSnapshot and storage state dump
Pool is crash-only: a wedged session is killed and replaced without taking the harness down
Per-task hard timeout kills the session and records SESSION_TIMEOUT
Smoke test: 8 tasks against the trivial agent run in parallel without cross-contamination of cookies/localStorage
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented at harness/ts/cdp/pool.ts. BrowserPool.create({size}) (default 4 / GBA_POOL_SIZE) launches N chromes upfront, each with its own --user-data-dir; acquire/release destroys+respawns the slot for true isolation. PooledBrowserSession adds snapshot()/restore() (URL + Network cookies + local/sessionStorage; optional MHTML audit payload via Page.captureSnapshot). pool.withSession(fn,{taskTimeoutMs}) SIGKILLs on deadline and throws SessionTimeoutError; agents map to terminal_state=SESSION_TIMEOUT. Crash-only: chrome exit handler replaces wedged slots without taking the pool down. Chrome is spawned detached:true and killed via process group (process.kill(-pid)) to prevent renderer subprocesses from racing the rm cleanup; this also fixed a pre-existing flake in smoke.test.ts. 21 TS tests (11 new in pool.test.ts) all pass; 3 Python tests still pass.

userStories.title: LLMClient with multi-provider, cost tracking, replay cache, and budget enforcement
userStories.description: As any agent, I need a unified LLM shim that talks to OpenAI and Gemini, records cost, supports replay-from-cache, and refuses calls that would exceed the per-task budget.
acceptanceCriteria: Unified async call(model, messages, **opts) signature; supports OpenAI Chat/Responses and Gemini
Records {model, prompt_hash, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, latency_ms, cost_usd} per call into the active Trajectory
Cache keyed by (model, prompt_hash, paradigm_seed); record mode persists, replay mode short-circuits
Per-task Budget object (tokens, usd, wall_seconds, steps) is checked before every call; exceeding raises BudgetExceeded which the harness records as BUDGET_EXCEEDED
Auth headers and API keys never appear in logs or trajectories
Tests cover record-then-replay determinism and budget refusal
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented at harness/ts/llm/{types,pricing,cache,client,index}.ts and providers/{openai,gemini}.ts. LLMClient routes by model prefix (gpt-*/o4-*/o3-* → OpenAI Chat Completions, gemini-* → Gemini generateContent), records {model, prompt_hash, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, latency_ms, cost_usd, cached} as a JSONL llm_call line in the active Trajectory, and persists deterministic cache entries under runs/.cache/llm/. Cache key is sha256(stable-json({model, messages, opts, paradigm_seed})). mode='record' (default) calls on miss + persists; mode='replay' throws LLMReplayMissError. Budget.check() runs before AND after each call. defaultClient(opts) wires providers from process.env (OPENAI_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY) and registers the keys for redaction. Python proxy at harness/python/gba_agent/llm.py forwards over the bridge; runPythonAgent({llm}) registers the llm.call handler. Tests: 15 new TS tests (cache hashing, record/replay determinism, replay-miss, budget refusal pre+post, secret redaction, cache content scrub, trajectory llm_call line, OpenAI/Gemini wire shape), 4 new Python tests (RPC round-trip, message dataclass, replay-miss exception translation, provider-unavailable translation). Total now: 36 TS + 7 Python passing.

userStories.title: Verifier framework (programmatic predicates + LLM-judge fallback)
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need every task to ship with a programmatic verifier and an optional LLM-judge fallback so success is measured the same way for every agent.
acceptanceCriteria: Verifier signature `verify(task, browser, trajectory) -> {pass: bool, score: 0..1, reason: str}` is enforced at task load
Programmatic verifiers run inside the page (CDP Runtime.evaluate) or against trajectory facts; preferred path
LLM-judge fallback uses temperature=0, n=3 majority vote, GPT-4o or equivalent, only when task is tagged judge_required
Verifier verdicts (pass/fail/score/reason) are themselves logged into the trajectory for audit
Sanity test: trivial agent fails non-trivial tasks; a hand-written 'cheat' agent passes them
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented at harness/ts/verifier/{types,yaml,loader,programmatic,llm_judge,runner,index}.ts. Three verifier kinds: js (CDP Runtime.evaluate in page, awaitPromise=true so bare booleans, Promises, and {pass,score,reason} objects all flow uniformly), trajectory_predicate (JS expression compiled with new Function and run against {steps, llmCalls, metadata} snapshots), llm_judge (temperature=0, n=3 majority vote with distinct paradigm_seed per call so all three responses cache independently; only allowed when task tagged judge_required). loadTaskFile validates the spec at load time — missing fields, unknown difficulty/kind, llm_judge without judge_required tag, or a trajectory_predicate that fails to compile all throw InvalidTaskSpecError. Trajectory.recordVerification appends a `verification` JSONL line; finish() folds the most-recent verification into metadata.verifier_verdict when no explicit verdict is given. verify(task, ctx) writes a verdict.json sidecar by default. Sanity test (real Chrome, localhost fixture): trivial click-first-link agent navigates to a decoy and the JS verifier reports pass:false; an inline cheat agent clicks the completion button and the same verifier reports pass:true. Minimal in-tree YAML loader (no dep) handles the subset task specs need. Total: 72 TS tests (36 new) + 7 Python tests passing.

userStories.notes: Added 3 new hostile fixtures: tasks/fixtures/pages/{modal_stack,conditional_form,iframe_drag}.ts and tasks/suite/hard/{modal-stack,conditional-form,iframe-drag}.yaml. Modal-stack is a 3-deep nested-modal state machine (idle->step1_done->step2_done->done) with decoy buttons that move the FSM to a frozen 'aborted' terminal — tagged irreversible. Conditional-form is a 4-step branching form: account_type (personal|business) determines which step-2 fields appear AND which regex validates them; country (usa|canada|mexico) determines which step-4 ID field validates; the server cross-checks the path on submit. Iframe-drag is parent + 2 same-origin child frames that relay mousedown (source) and mouseup (target) via window.parent.postMessage; parent's listener records {sourceId,targetId,x,y,droppedAt} into window.__test.drops. Hard slice now has 6 fixtures; trivial click-first-link fails ALL six (all DECLINED), hand-crafted cheats pass all six. 91 TS tests (7 new: 1 conditional HTTP round-trip + 6 sanity tests) + 7 Python tests passing.

userStories.title: Hard task suite v3 (4 final brutal fixtures, 10 total)
userStories.description: Round out the hard slice with fixtures for late hydration, multi-tab workflows, recoverable failure, and PDF-then-summarise.
acceptanceCriteria: Fixture 7: late-hydration SPA where the visible button is non-functional for the first 1500ms; verifier requires post-hydration click
Fixture 8: multi-tab workflow where clicking 'open report' spawns a new tab with the answer; verifier checks data was carried back
Fixture 9: recoverable failure - submit endpoint returns 500 once before succeeding; verifier checks final 200 receipt
Fixture 10: download a PDF and answer a question about its contents; verifier checks the answer string
Hard slice is now exactly 10 fixtures; reference solution passes >=8 of 10
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Added 4 final hostile fixtures; hard slice is now exactly 10. tasks/fixtures/pages/{late_hydration,multi_tab,recoverable,pdf_task}.ts + tasks/suite/hard/{late-hydration,multi-tab,recoverable,pdf-task}.yaml. late-hydration: Confirm button has no real handler for the first 1500ms; pre-hydration clicks bump window.__test.attempts and surface a 'Still loading…' banner but don't advance. Verifier requires window.__test.completed===true AND clickedAt>=hydratedAt. multi-tab: parent page generates a per-load token, the 'Open report' button calls window.open('/multi-tab/report?token=…'); popup fetches a per-token code from /__multitab/report and posts it back via window.opener.postMessage, then window.close()s. Submit cross-checks code against per-token expected value server-side. Cheat bypasses popup (fetches code directly using window.__test.token) — real agents need either popup support or this fast path. recoverable: POST /__recoverable/submit returns 500 on first attempt per session, 200 after; page surfaces banner + re-enables submit so an agent that retries succeeds. Verifier requires j.ok===true and attempts>=2. pdf-task: GET /report.pdf serves a minimal valid 5-object PDF (~400 bytes, opens in real PDF viewers) whose text run is 'Quarterly access code is XXXXXXXX'; access code regenerated on every /__reset via randomAccessCode(). Cheat fetches the PDF, latin1-decodes, regex-extracts the answer, submits. Trivial click-first-link FAILs all 10 hard fixtures (DECLINED on 9 of 10; DONE on pdf-task because it navigates to the PDF link but never submits). All 10 cheat agents PASS — satisfies AC #5 (>=8/10). 103 TS tests + 7 Python tests passing (12 new TS: 5 fixtures_server.test.ts cases + 8 fixtures_sanity.test.ts cases minus 1 net for the consolidation).

userStories.title: Resumable tournament runner with budget enforcement
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want one command to run all agents × all tasks × N seeds with per-task budgets, resumable across restarts, that emits a structured leaderboard.
acceptanceCriteria: `make tournament` loads agents from agents/, tasks from tasks/suite/<slice>/, runs each (agent, task, seed) cell
Per-task budgets enforced: easy(50k tokens, $0.20, 60s, 15 steps), medium(200k, $1.00, 240s, 40 steps), hard(600k, $3.00, 600s, 80 steps)
Resumable: a (agent, task, seed) cell with a completed trajectory is NOT re-executed
Emits runs/leaderboard.json with per-agent rows: {success_pct, mean_steps, mean_cost_usd, p50_latency_ms, p95_latency_ms, recovery_count, decline_count}
Single-elimination logic supported by a CLI flag (--bracket=on)
Tournament completes the trivial+baseline pair on the easy slice without manual intervention
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented at harness/ts/tournament/{types,discovery,summary,leaderboard,bracket,runner,index}.ts and harness/ts/cli/tournament.ts. discoverAgents() scans agents/<id>/ for manifest.yaml + agent.{ts,py}, validates the manifest (id/language/summary required), and skips invalid entries with a warning instead of aborting. Per-cell done-marker is `runs/<agent>/<task>/<seed>/summary.json` — the runner reads it back into the leaderboard without ever gunzipping the trajectory; absence triggers a fresh run. summary.json carries {agent_id, task_id, seed, difficulty, terminal_state, pass, score, reason, decline_reason, steps, llm_calls, cost_usd, tokens_in, tokens_out, latency_ms, attempts, completed_at}. Per-task budgets reuse DIFFICULTY_BUDGETS from harness/ts/eval/runner.ts (easy/medium/hard tiers from AC #2). aggregate() builds per-agent rows with the documented metrics; sort order matches the champion tiebreaker (success_pct desc, mean_cost_usd asc, p95_latency_ms asc). buildBracket() does snake-seeded single-elimination and is opt-in via `--bracket=on`; the result lands at slices.<slice>.bracket in leaderboard.json. CLI accepts --slice (csv), --seeds, --agents (csv filter), --tasks (csv filter), --bracket, --retries, --runs-root. Tests: 26 new TS tests across tournament_{discovery,leaderboard,bracket,summary,resume}.test.ts — discovery (live + synthetic dirs + manifest validation), leaderboard (percentiles, recovery_count, decline_count, sort tiebreakers, multi-slice + overall), bracket (2/3/4 agents, ties, alphabetical seeding), summary I/O round trip, end-to-end resume against the trivial agent on the hard fixture (asserts the leaderboard.json shape, the summary.json sidecar, and that re-entry runs ZERO cells). 148 TS tests + 7 Python tests passing.

userStories.title: Agent auto-discovery and contract tests
userStories.description: As Ralph, I need to drop a new agent into agents/<id>/ and have the harness pick it up automatically with no central registry edit, so adding agents stays cheap.
acceptanceCriteria: Convention: agents/<id>/ contains agent.ts (or agent.py) exporting a default Agent subclass and a manifest.yaml ({id, language, summary, approach_keywords, distinct_from})
Harness scans agents/ on tournament start; missing or invalid agents are reported but do not abort the run
Contract test runs each agent on a 1-task dry slice and rejects agents that do not produce a Trajectory
manifest.distinct_from is enforced: a new agent declaring distinct_from=['agent-x'] is rejected if its approach_keywords overlap >50% with agent-x's
Adding a no-op agent under agents/ requires zero edits outside that directory
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented at harness/ts/tournament/{distinctness,contract}.ts. Distinctness uses Jaccard(approach_keywords, target_keywords) >0.5 (case-insensitive set comparison); empty lists return 0. validateDistinctness(agents) returns one DistinctnessIssue per (violator, target) pair; discoverAgents post-passes the issues, drops violators, and emits formatDistinctnessWarning(issue) so a tournament never silently runs an agent whose distinctness claim is untrue. Opt out via enforceDistinctness:false. Self-references and references to agents not in the discovery set are skipped silently. runContractTest({agents, runsRoot, browserFactory?}) runs each agent on a 1-task dry slice (default: tiny data: URL, default budget = 1k tokens / $1 / 30s / 5 steps) and validates the produced trajectory using DUCK TYPING (looks for `.metadata` and `.isFinished`), NOT `instanceof Trajectory` — tsx may load an agent file via a different module URL than the harness's static import, which gives false negatives across the boundary. Captures one ContractResult{agent_id, ok, reason, terminal_state} per agent; failures don't abort the loop. Tests: 27 new (14 distinctness unit/integration, 7 contract incl. live agents + tmpdir TS no-op + tmpdir Python no-op + 4 failure modes). 189 TS tests + 7 Python tests passing. agents/CLAUDE.md distinctness section now says 'enforced' and documents the contract test.

acceptanceCriteria: agents/baseline-a11y-react/ implements a complete end-to-end agent: a11y snapshot via CDP, interactive-element labelling, ReAct loop, action set {click, type, scroll, wait, navigate, extract, finish}
Agent runs end-to-end on >=15/20 easy tasks and >=1/10 hard tasks

userStories.notes: Implemented at agents/baseline-a11y-react/{agent,actions,snapshot}.ts + manifest.yaml + README.md. The agent is the honest control: each step takes one in-page snapshot via Runtime.evaluate that walks every interactive element, marks it with a stable data-gba-aid attribute, and surfaces {role, accessible name, type/value/placeholder/href, visible/disabled} to the LLM as a numbered list. A single LLM turn (default gpt-4o-mini, temperature=0) emits one JSON action; the parser tolerates ```json fences + leading prose but rejects unknown action types. Action set: click(target) / type(target,text,submit?) / scroll(direction,pixels?) / wait(ms) / navigate(url) / extract(query) / finish(reason). LLM-unavailable + replay-miss both resolve to a clean DECLINED so the contract test runs without secrets. Browser-use, Stagehand, AgentE cited in README; differences (text-only, no skill library, no self-critique) called out. AGENT_ALIASES.baseline now points at baseline-a11y-react (was a click-first-link placeholder per the US-009 comment). Tests: 18 new in baseline_agent.test.ts (parser tolerance + edge cases, formatSnapshot rendering, real-Chrome ReAct loop with a scripted mock provider clicking aid=1 then finishing, type+submit form submission, no-LLM DECLINED path, tight-budget BUDGET_EXCEEDED short-circuit, snapshot tags interactive elements with monotonic data-gba-aid that persist across calls). Smoke: `eval AGENT=baseline SLICE=easy SEEDS=1` runs end-to-end on all 22 easy tasks (19/22 PASS without an LLM because the runner navigates first and easy verifiers check page content; 3 fail because they require interaction). 207 TS tests + 7 Python tests passing.

userStories.title: Agent slot 1 — first novel complete agent
userStories.description: As Ralph, I will design and ship a COMPLETE end-to-end browser agent under an approach of my choosing. userStories.notes: Implemented at agents/runtime-codegen/{agent,codegen,observe}.ts + manifest.yaml + README.md. userStories.notes: Implemented at agents/speculative-rollback/{agent,actions,observe,snapshot}.ts + manifest.yaml + README.md. Mechanism: every action is a SPECULATIVE TRIAL. Each step the agent (1) observes the page and captures a client-side state snapshot (URL + localStorage + sessionStorage via Runtime.evaluate; HttpOnly cookies and server side-effects deliberately out of scope), (2) asks the PROPOSER LLM for K=2 candidate CSS-selector-keyed actions in a JSON envelope, (3) executes the top candidate, (4) asks the JUDGE LLM (separate temperature=0 call) to classify the post-action state as commit/revert/done, (5) on revert, calls restoreState (clear+rewrite storage, then navigate back to snapshot URL), adds the action's label to a per-state blacklist, tries the next candidate. The blacklist is reset on commit; on done, finish DONE. Distinct from all prior agents on the loop axis: baseline-a11y-react has no rollback, plan-then-execute commits the whole plan upfront, runtime-codegen has neither a judge nor snapshot/restore. approach_keywords=[speculative_execution, state_snapshot_restore, progress_judging, dual_llm_judge, revert_on_regret, branch_pruning]; Jaccard=0 vs all three priors (distinct_from lists all three). 27 new tests in speculative_rollback_agent.test.ts: parseCandidates tolerance (envelope/array/object/fences/prose/action-wrapper, unknown-type/missing-selector/empty-list rejection, wait+scroll edge cases), parseJudge (commit/revert/done parse, invalid verdict rejection, fence tolerance), captureState/restoreState on the fixtures server (storage round-trip across a simulated revert, opaque-origin no-throw), end-to-end real-Chrome runs (single-candidate commit→finish, two-candidate revert-then-commit with verified __clicked='good', immediate done-after-first verdict, parse_error tolerance, no-LLM DECLINED, tight-budget BUDGET_EXCEEDED), manifest distinctness vs all three prior agents. userStories.notes: Implemented at agents/predicate-driven/{agent,predicate,actions,observe}.ts + manifest.yaml + README.md. Mechanism: ONE upfront LLM synthesises a JS PREDICATE that returns true in the page when the goal is satisfied; the agent loop terminates from CODE the moment the predicate evaluates true after an action. Distinct on the TERMINATION axis from all four prior agents — every other agent gives the LLM the final word (baseline `finish`, plan-then-execute end-of-plan, runtime-codegen body.done, srb judge done); this one inverts that. The action LLM has NO `finish` action — actions.parseAction explicitly rejects {type:"finish"} with a message about the agent's invariant. Predicate.ts wraps the LLM-emitted expression as `(async () => { try { return Boolean(<expr>); } catch (e) { return { __predicate_error: ... }; } })()` so syntax errors fall out of evaluate() (parse fails before try) and runtime errors come back as a typed object — evaluatePredicate normalises both into {satisfied: bool, error?: string}. Agent's predicate is separate from the harness verifier (harness/ts/verifier/); they may even disagree, which costs the agent steps but never leaks signal into scoring. approach_keywords=[goal_predicate, predicate_loop, llm_synthesised_test, code_terminated_loop, self_verifying, condition_polling]; Jaccard=0 vs all four prior agents. Live eval (gpt-4o-mini, temperature=0): easy 18/22 PASS in 111.1s (4 fails: easy-httpbin-form / easy-httpbin-html / easy-rfc-791 / easy-wiki-linux — agent's predicate was too narrow and it navigated away from start_url breaking the verifier's regex match), hard 2/10 PASS in 107.0s (modal-stack and recoverable). Both ≥3/20 easy and ≥1/10 hard thresholds met with margin. Modal-stack is a new pass driven by the mechanism: the predicate keeps the agent honest about hitting all 3 modal-FSM transitions instead of stopping after one click. Recoverable is the canonical predicate-driven win — the loop simply does not exit until the post-500-retry submit lands, where prior agents either declared `finish` after the first 500 (baseline / plan-then-execute) or got there via the proposer/judge path (srb). README cites prior failure traces from runs/baseline-a11y-react/hard-recoverable, runs/plan-then-execute/hard-recoverable, runs/baseline-a11y-react/hard-late-hydration. 313 TS tests + 7 Python tests passing (33 new in predicate_driven_agent.test.ts: parsePredicate alternates + rejection paths, parseAction rejects finish, evaluatePredicate truthy/falsy/runtime-error/syntax-error/async paths, end-to-end real-Chrome runs covering predicate-true-at-start, predicate-fires-after-click, max-steps DECLINED, action-LLM-emits-finish recorded as parse_error, synthesiser-unparseable DECLINED, no-LLM DECLINED, tight-budget BUDGET_EXCEEDED, manifest distinctness vs all four prior agents). Required harness changes: (1) LLMMessage.content extended to `string | LLMContentPart[]` with new LLMContentPart union mirroring OpenAI's text/image_url shape; cache key handles arrays via existing stableSort; Gemini provider throws on array content. (2) OpenAI provider gained 429 + 5xx retry-with-backoff (parses Retry-After header AND body's 'try again in Xms' hint, default maxRetries=5 capped at 30s). (3) vision-grounded agent's loop adds a 200ms post-action settle delay AND threads a step counter into the prompt so identical-looking screenshots don't trap the agent in a cache-stuck loop. userStories.notes: Implemented at agents/network-shadow/{agent,actions,network,observe}.ts + manifest.yaml + README.md. Mechanism: API-first browser agent. A fetch + XMLHttpRequest monkey-patch is installed at run start via BOTH Page.addScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument (covers future docs/popups) and Runtime.evaluate (covers the already-loaded doc since the harness navigates to start_url before agent.run()). Every page-issued and agent-issued HTTP request lands in window.__gba_net_log (capped at 60 entries FIFO with ~600-char body samples). Each step the LLM sees a 12-entry tail alongside a minimal page summary (URL, title, forms with action+method+field-names, visible buttons) and emits one of: fetch (method, url, body?, content_type? — executed in-page so cookies+origin are preserved), click (UI fallback to make the page reveal its own endpoints), navigate, wait, done, decline. Distinct from every prior slot on TWO axes simultaneously: observation modality (network traffic vs DOM/a11y for baseline/p-t-e/srb/predicate-driven, pixels for vision-grounded, raw text for runtime-codegen) AND action substrate (HTTP requests vs aids/text/CSS-selectors/raw-JS/pixel-coords). approach_keywords=[network_introspection, api_first, http_actions, traffic_logging, request_replay, fetch_xhr_monkeypatch] — Jaccard=0 vs all six prior agents (asserted explicitly in the manifest test). Idempotency: the patch's IIFE guards with `if (window.__gba_net_installed) return;` so a second install on the same document is a no-op. XHR wrap preserves the prototype + own properties so pages doing `xhr instanceof XMLHttpRequest` still work. Live eval (gpt-4o-mini, temperature=0): easy 21/22 PASS in 141.8s (only easy-httpbin-form fails — same labelless-input form that defeats plan-then-execute; the agent's UI fallback lacks label-finder semantics); hard 3/10 PASS in 88.0s — hard-modal-stack, hard-recoverable, hard-shadow-form. Hard-shadow-form is the canonical API-first result: the agent POSTed JSON directly to /__shadow/submit without ever traversing the shadow DOM. Hard-recoverable is the loop's natural retry-after-500 win. Both AC thresholds (≥3/20 easy, ≥1/10 hard) met with margin. The 7 hard failures are substrate-bound (canvas-drag/iframe-drag verifiers check window.__test, not a server endpoint), LLM composition limits (conditional-form, late-hydration, pdf-task, multi-tab), or out-of-vocabulary (virtual-scroll's id enumeration). 27 new TS tests in network_shadow_agent.test.ts: parseAction for all 6 actions (fetch/click/navigate/wait/done/decline) + tolerance (fences/prose) + error paths (unknown type, missing fields, empty); actionLabel rendering; real-Chrome installPatch + readNetLog round-trip capturing a page-issued POST; idempotent double-install (only one entry per request); opaque-origin data: URL no-throw; executeAction fetch round-trip + click + click-no-match + wait; end-to-end runs with scripted LLM covering fetch+done DONE path, parse_error mid-loop recovery, no-LLM DECLINED, tight-budget BUDGET_EXCEEDED; manifest distinctness vs ALL six prior agents (zero keyword overlap). 416 TS tests + 7 Python tests passing (27 new). Iteration 2 hardened the infrastructure AND ran the deferred live sweep. **Hardening (iter 2):** (1) vikunja-init service (alpine:3 chowning the named volume to uid 1000) prevents fresh-volume permission-denied restart loop. (2) bookstack DB env vars renamed DB_USER/DB_PASS → DB_USERNAME/DB_PASSWORD to match what lscr.io/linuxserver/bookstack expects (silently falls back to 'database_username' default otherwise, 500's on first migration). (3) bookstack APP_URL changed http://localhost:3003 → http://127.0.0.1:3003 to match what we navigate to — mismatching origins make in-page fetch('/login') cross-origin on the redirect, surfacing as 'TypeError: Failed to fetch' from loginAs(bookstack). (4) seed_bookstack.sh updated \BookStack\Auth\{User,Role} → \BookStack\Users\Models\{User,Role} for BookStack 24+ class layout. (5) Verifier wrap fix: harness/ts/verifier/programmatic.ts wrapForRuntimeEvaluate now strips trailing whitespace + `;` — without this, the idiomatic multi-line `|` YAML block scalar shape `(async () => { ... })();\n` (every hard-app verifier) produced invalid `(...;)` and surfaced as 'js verifier threw: Uncaught' on EVERY hard-app cell. 3 new regression tests at harness/ts/tests/verifier_iife_wrap.test.ts. (6) BookStack create-page task tightened (AC #5): verifier now requires BOTH 'owner: ralph' phrase AND a fenced code block (`<pre>` or `<code>`) in the rendered page HTML; new contract test asserts this. (7) tasks/CLAUDE.md gained Outline-vs-BookStack rationale + vikunja-init permissions documentation. **Live sweep (AC #8):** All 8 LLM agents + click-first-link ran end-to-end against all 9 cells in ~13 minutes (up to 7 parallel tournament processes, total cost <$1 in OpenAI tokens). Zero harness crashes. Final scores: predicate-driven 5/9 (55.6%), vision-grounded 5/9 (55.6%), runtime-codegen 4/9, dom-mutation-stream 4/9, speculative-rollback 4/9, plan-then-execute 3/9, baseline-a11y-react 2/9, network-shadow 1/9. Full per-task PASS matrix + per-agent observations at docs/hard-app-sweep-2026-05-10.md. Notable: vision-grounded jumped from 0/10 hard fixtures to 5/9 hard-app — empirical evidence that the synthetic hostile fixtures over-index on substrate failures real apps don't share, and a strong argument for the hard-app slice as the better generalisation test. State contamination caveat documented (verifiers check app REST state not provenance; trivial agent scored 4/9 in a post-sweep re-run because earlier LLM agents had already created the artifacts) — does not affect AC #8 (which explicitly does not constrain pass rate) but US-022/US-023 will need either per-cell state reset OR per-agent salts. 459 TS tests + 7 Python tests passing (4 new in verifier_iife_wrap.test.ts + hard_app_slice.test.ts code-block assertion). Files changed: harness/ts/verifier/programmatic.ts, harness/ts/tests/verifier_iife_wrap.test.ts (new), harness/ts/tests/hard_app_slice.test.ts, tasks/suite/hard-app/hard-app-bookstack-create-page.yaml, tasks/CLAUDE.md, infra/docker/docker-compose.yml, infra/docker/seed/seed_bookstack.sh, infra/docker/TODO.md, docs/hard-app-sweep-2026-05-10.md (new), prd.json, progress.txt.

userStories.title: Easy slice v2 — multi-step interactive replacement
userStories.description: As Ralph, replace the trivial 'open URL → regex on body' easy tasks with multi-step interactive tasks that still pass in <60s for a competent agent. Today's easy slice gives almost no signal between mechanisms; this fixes the compression.
acceptanceCriteria: tasks/suite/easy/ retains ~22 yaml files but at least 14 of them require >=3 interactions (click, type, scroll, navigate) before the verifier can pass. Pure-extraction tasks are capped at 8 (kept as canary tests).
No task uses the same fixture-pattern twice (e.g. only one wikipedia search-and-extract).
Each task's verifier still runs in <500ms after agent.run() returns.
Easy slice continues to be hosted on real public sites (none on local fixtures — that's the hard slice's job).
Trivial click-first-link agent passes <=4/22 of the new easy slice (proves the slice is no longer trivially satisfiable).
Existing 7 agents are re-evaluated; results land at docs/easy-v2-results-<date>.md.
tasks/CLAUDE.md updated.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Replaced all 22 easy tasks. Slice is now 8 canaries (extract skill + canary tag) + 14 interactive (search/navigate/fill skill + interactive tag) across 10 distinct hosts (example.com, en.wikipedia.org, www.rfc-editor.org, developer.mozilla.org, arxiv.org, httpbin.org, www.iana.org, info.cern.ch, github.com, www.python.org). Each task carries a unique pattern:<id> tag enforced by the validator. Canary verifiers strictly pin document.location.pathname === <start_url_path> AND document.location.hash === ''; interactive verifiers REQUIRE a document.location regex match AND a /.../i case-insensitive body regex (mirrors the hard-real invariant). The 14 interactive patterns are: wiki_search_box, wiki_inline_link, wiki_action_tab, mdn_see_also_link, mdn_toc_anchor, rfc_format_link, arxiv_listing_click, github_repo_issues_tab, github_repo_file_click, github_repo_pulls_tab, iana_nav_link, cern_nested_link, httpbin_form_submit, python_nav_link. AC #5 verified live: click-first-link scores 4/22 = 18.2% (=AC ceiling). Three of the four passes are canaries whose start_url has a skip-to-content anchor or no <a> tags (arxiv-bert, mdn-array-map, httpbin-headers); the fourth (cern-nested-page) is a coincidental hit where the first link on info.cern.ch IS the task's target. Live sweep of 8 LLM agents (gpt-4o-mini, temp=0, easy-tier budget): network-shadow 19/22 (86.4%, top), predicate-driven/baseline-a11y-react/speculative-rollback 17/22 (77.3%), plan-then-execute/dom-mutation-stream/runtime-codegen 16/22 (72.7%), vision-grounded 8/22 (36.4% — pixel-coord centre-bias on dense real-site nav bars). ~12-point spread vs the pre-US-029 4-point spread is the slice's new signal. Total sweep cost ~$2.49 across 176 cells in ~16 min wall clock (8 parallel tournament processes). Full per-task PASS matrix + per-agent observations at docs/easy-v2-results-2026-05-11.md. easy_slice.test.ts grew from 10 → 15 tests (+5: bucket marker, canary count, interactive count, interactive verifier shape, pattern uniqueness). 464 TS tests + 7 Python tests passing (5 net new; 5 reshaped). tasks/CLAUDE.md §Easy slice section rewritten as 'Easy slice v2' with bucket conventions + 'Why two buckets?' rationale. Files changed: 22 easy yamls (delete 13, update 9, add 13), harness/ts/tests/easy_slice.test.ts, tasks/CLAUDE.md, docs/easy-v2-results-2026-05-11.md (new), prd.json, progress.txt.

userStories.title: Vision agent v2 — Set-of-Marks (successor to vision-grounded)
userStories.description: As Ralph, ship a vision agent that fixes the documented failure mode of vision-grounded by overlaying numbered DOM-derived bounding boxes on the screenshot. The LLM selects targets by integer mark id, not (x,y) pixel guesses. This is the canonical fix WebVoyager / SeeAct / Operator use; it is the only known way for non-purpose-trained chat models to ground vision actions reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: agents/vision-som/ implements the agent.

Per step: collect interactive elements from the DOM (role-aware), draw numbered rectangles on the screenshot via canvas (or via Page.addStyleSheet + a sibling overlay div captured into the screenshot), send {annotated_image, mark_table} to the LLM.
LLM picks a mark id and an action (click/type/scroll/wait/done); harness translates to a CDP action against the stable element id behind the mark — never raw pixel coords.
manifest.distinct_from includes vision-grounded with note 'successor with Set-of-Marks fix'; approach_keywords differ enough that the Jaccard distinctness check passes.
Live eval on hard slice: >=3/10 PASS (3x the failed vision-grounded baseline) OR documented analysis of which mark-extraction heuristic still misses targets.
Live eval on easy v2 slice (US-029) once available: >=14/22 PASS.
README cites at least 2 vision-grounded trajectories that motivated the SoM fix.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Composed agent — runtime-codegen × predicate-driven (successor to speculative-rollback)
userStories.description: As Ralph, ship a composed agent that fuses the highest-hard-score mechanism (runtime-codegen, raw-JS body per step) with the most distinctive termination mechanism (predicate-driven, code-decided 'done'). One LLM call per step (vs speculative-rollback's two), but the loop still terminates from CODE — closing the cost/quality gap that killed speculative-rollback. The COMPOSITION is the novelty: neither agent in isolation has both a free-form action substrate AND a code-terminated loop.
acceptanceCriteria: agents/codegen-predicate/ implements the agent.
ONE upfront synthesis LLM call emits a JS predicate function that returns true when the goal is satisfied (reuses agents/predicate-driven/predicate.ts wrapper).

Per step: action LLM emits a JS body (reuses agents/runtime-codegen/agent.ts execution path); after the body returns, harness evaluates the predicate. If true, loop exits with terminal_state='DONE_BY_PREDICATE' — the action LLM has no `done` action.

manifest.distinct_from includes runtime-codegen, predicate-driven, AND speculative-rollback. README justifies the composition as new vs the union (the LLM authors free-form code AND the loop's exit condition is independent of the LLM's self-report).

Live eval on hard slice: >=6/10 PASS (1 more than runtime-codegen's 5/10 — the predicate-driven termination should claw back at least one fixture, e.g. recoverable). If <6/10, README explains why the composition didn't compound.
Live eval on easy slice >=20/22.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: DOM-as-filesystem agent (shell-style action vocabulary)
userStories.description: As Ralph, ship an agent whose action substrate is a tiny shell-style command vocabulary over the DOM tree treated as a filesystem. Distinct from runtime-codegen (which is unbounded JS) AND from baseline (which is named JSON actions): the LLM emits a single shell line per turn, the harness parses it into one of {ls, cd, cat, grep, find, attr, click, type, scroll, wait, done} and compiles each to a small Runtime.evaluate. The cwd is a CSS selector path that persists across turns — letting the LLM 'navigate into' a subtree (e.g. into a shadow root or iframe) and operate there with terse local-relative selectors.
acceptanceCriteria: agents/dom-shell/ implements the agent.
Command grammar documented in README.md with examples; parser is tolerant (one command per turn, args may be quoted).
ls returns a compact listing of children with role + first 20 chars of innerText. find supports a --interactive flag (filters to button/a/input/textarea/select).
cwd is maintained per-step as a stable selector chain; cd .. pops, cd / resets. cwd survives navigations only if the selector still resolves.
cat reads element.innerText (capped at 4000 chars); attr reads a single attribute; grep filters cat output.
Action commands (click/type/scroll) operate on cwd-relative selectors; the harness resolves them via document.querySelector(cwd + ' ' + arg).
manifest.distinct_from covers ALL prior agents; approach_keywords clearly differentiate (filesystem_metaphor, cwd_navigation, shell_command_substrate, vocabulary_compiled_to_evaluate).
Live eval: >=18/22 easy v2 (US-029), >=4/10 hard. The filesystem metaphor should claw back conditional-form and shadow-form via cd into the subtree.
Trajectory step records carry `cwd` so post-hoc analysis can replay the navigation.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

steeringNotes.summary: User reviewed first-7-agent results and steered the project toward (a) harder + more REAL-WORLD tasks, (b) creative mechanism compositions and DOM-as-filesystem-style novelty, (c) replacing under-performing agents instead of stacking more variants, (d) model diversity beyond gpt-4o-mini, (e) the mixture meta-agent that answers 'best agent possible'.

deprioritizedAgents.reason: 0/10 hard. Pure pixel coordinate emission is a documented structural failure mode of un-augmented chat models (centre-bias on x). Keep the existing trajectories as data, but DO NOT clone this mechanism into a new slot. Successor is US-031 (vision + Set-of-Marks).

1/10 hard despite paying ~2x LLM cost per step (proposer + judge). The judge call dominates cost without expanding the mechanism's capability frontier. Keep the trajectories, but DO NOT clone the proposer/judge loop into a new slot. Successor is US-032 (rollback-style termination wrapped around the runtime-codegen mechanism, single LLM call per step).

doNotBuildAgain: Pure pixel-coordinate vision agents without overlay augmentation (SoM, coordinate grid, or purpose-trained GUI model).
Two-LLM-call-per-step loops where the second call is a generic 'did that help?' judge — pay once for an objective predicate instead.
Raw-JS-bodied agents that duplicate runtime-codegen's substrate (US-015 already owns it; new agents must pick a DIFFERENT action primitive).
preferredDirections: DOM-as-filesystem: an LLM navigates the DOM via shell-style commands (cd / ls / cat / grep / find / attr / click / type) where each command compiles to a tiny Runtime.evaluate. The action vocabulary is the LLM's whole API — distinct from runtime-codegen (which is unbounded JS) AND from baseline (which is named JSON actions). See US-033.

Mechanism compositions where the composition itself is the novelty: e.g. runtime-codegen body × predicate-driven termination (US-032), or network-shadow observation × baseline action set, or vision-SoM perception × runtime-codegen action.
Set-of-Marks vision (US-031): numbered DOM-derived overlay → LLM picks integer id → harness translates to CDP click. The proven fix for un-augmented vision agents.

Skill crystallisation / Voyager-for-the-web: agent records successful trajectories as named skills, retrieves them on similar tasks. Currently no agent has any persistent learning — every run starts cold.

Filesystem-as-working-memory: the agent has a real on-disk scratch (notes.md, plan.md, observations/) that survives across steps — Karpathy-style. Distinct from in-prompt scratchpads because the context window doesn't grow linearly.
rigourGaps: Hard slice is 100% local fixtures. Add real public sites (US-026) AND self-hosted complex apps (US-027) to test transfer.
Easy slice is mostly 'open URL + regex on body'. Replace with ≥3-step interactive flows (US-029).
Only one LLM (gpt-4o-mini) is in use; we cannot tell mechanism from model. Wire Claude (US-030) and re-run the top-2 agents under ≥3 models.
No mixture meta-agent — the obvious 'best agent possible' answer. US-024 must run after the new infra/agent stories settle.
steeringNotes.orderingHint: Suggested execution order after US-020 finishes: US-026 → US-027 → US-029 → US-030 → US-031 → US-032 → US-033 → US-021 (free Ralph slot) → US-028 (opt-in auth) → US-022 (failure mining) → US-023 (full tournament) → US-024 (mixture meta-agent) → US-025 (post-mortem). Priority numbers reflect this order.
